title: Ensure that encryption providers are appropriately configured

input_row.title: Ensure that encryption providers are appropriately configured
input_row.description: Where etcd encryption is used, appropriate providers should be configured.
input_row.rationale: Where etcd encryption is used, it is important to ensure that the appropriate set of encryption providers is used. Currently, the aescbc , kms and secretbox are likely to be appropriate options.
input_row.audit: OpenShift supports encryption of data at rest of etcd datastore, but it is up to the customer to configure. The asecbc cipher had been the only supported cipher up to OCP 4.13. Starting with OCP 4.13, the aescgm cipher can be used as well. No other ciphers are supported. Keys are stored on the filesystem of the master and automatically rotated. Run the following command to review the Encrypted status condition for the OpenShift API server to verify that its resources were successfully encrypted: # encrypt the etcd datastore oc get openshiftapiserver -o=jsonpath='{range .items[0].status.conditions[?(@.type=="Encrypted")]}{.reason}{"\n"}{.message}{"\n"}' The output shows EncryptionCompleted upon successful encryption. • EncryptionCompleted • All resources encrypted: routes.route.openshift.io, oauthaccesstokens.oauth.openshift.io, oauthauthorizetokens.oauth.openshift.io If the output shows EncryptionInProgress , this means that encryption is still in progress. Wait a few minutes and try again.
input_row.remediation: Follow the OpenShift documentation for encrypting etcd data .
input_row.impact: When you enable etcd encryption, the following OpenShift API server and Kubernetes API server resources are encrypted: • Secrets • ConfigMaps • Routes • OAuth access tokens • OAuth authorize tokens When you enable etcd encryption, encryption keys are created. These keys are rotated on a weekly basis. You must have these keys in order to restore from an etcd backup.
input_row.default_value: By default, no encryption provider is set.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Access OpenShift API:** Use your web browser or tools like `oc` command line to access the OpenShift API.
   - Command: `oc get openshiftapiserver -o=jsonpath='{range .items[0].status.conditions[?(@.type==\"Encrypted\")]}{.reason}{\"\\n\"}{.message}{\"\\n\"}'`
2. **Review the Output:**
   - Verify the output of the command. Look for the "reason" and "message".
3. **Expected Secure Configuration:**
   - The output should indicate "EncryptionCompleted" meaning encryption is fully set up.
   - Resources encrypted should include: secrets, config maps, routes, OAuth access tokens, OAuth authorize tokens.
4. **Confirm Compliance:**
   - If the message states "EncryptionInProgress", continue to monitor till it changes to "EncryptionCompleted". If "EncryptionCompleted" is shown, it indicates that etcd encryption is configured appropriately, thus confirming compliance.
